zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard Coleman於1590年5月30日出生在英國艾可格亞特 布拉泰德時，他的父親Sir James Coleman21歲，母親Mary Elizabeth Spencer19歲。他於1611年6月25日與Alice Sowtherne在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾米可萊頓結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1679年5月13日在英國英格蘭素福爾可布倫特 艾萊享年88歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭素福爾可布倫特 艾萊。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard Coleman于1590年5月30日出生在英国艾可格亞特 布拉泰德时，他的父亲Sir James Coleman21岁，母亲Mary Elizabeth Spencer19岁。他于1611年6月25日与Alice Sowtherne在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾米可萊頓结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1679年5月13日在英国英格兰素福爾可布倫特 艾萊享年88岁，埋葬在英国英格兰素福爾可布倫特 艾萊。

nameMeanings.name: Coleman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish and English: from the Middle English personal name Col(e)man, Old Irish Colmán, earlier Columbán, adopted as Old Norse Kalman. It was introduced into Cumbria, Westmorland, and Yorkshire by Norwegians from Ireland and probably spread widely across England. Ó Colmáin (‘descendant of Colmán’) was the name of an Irish missionary to Europe, also known as Saint Columban(us) (c. 540–615), who founded the monastery of Bobbio in northern Italy in 614. Columbanus is formally a derivative of the Latin for ‘dove’, seen in the name of the 6th-century missionary known in English as Saint Columba (521–597), who converted the Picts to Christianity. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: from Mac Colmáin or Ó Colmáin ‘son (or descendant) of Colmán’.
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

variants.name: Kalman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Bela, Gabor, Sandor, Imre, Lajos, Geza, Gyula, Katalin, Zoltan.

Hungarian (Kálmán): from the old Hungarian personal name Kálmán, meaning ‘remainder’ (from Turkic kal ‘to remain’), hence a protective name, which was given to infants to ward off evil and harmful spirits. In medieval times the name became particularly popular because it was confused with the German Christian name Koloman of Irish origin (see Kolman 2).

Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish personal name Kalmen, an everyday form of Kloynemes (from Hebrew Kalonimos, which is from Greek kalos ‘lovely’ or kallos ‘beauty’ + onyma ‘name’). This Hebrew name is first recorded in the Talmud and has been used continuously since then. Among Hungarian Jews, it was sometimes confused with 1 above.

variants.name: Colman
meanings: Irish (Mayo and Sligo) and English: variant of   Coleman  .
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kohlmann   or   Kollmann  .
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

variants.name: Clifford
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from any of various places called Clifford in Devon, Gloucestershire, and Yorkshire, and in particular Herefordshire. The placename is derived from Old English clif ‘slope’ + ford ‘ford’.

Irish: adoption of the name in 1 above as Anglicization of several Irish names, especially Ó Clúmháin ‘descendant of Clúmhán’, which was the surname of an ecclesiastical family in Sligo and can be traced back to the 12th century. The personal name meant ‘little hairy one’, a diminutive of Irish clúmach ‘hairy’, from clúmh ‘feathers, plumage, down; hair or fur’. Clifford was also adopted for Coleman and in Fermanagh for Crifferty, Clifferty, and Cliffordy, which are Anglicized forms of Mac Raibheartaigh (compare Rafferty ).

History: A powerful Anglo-Norman family of this name in England and Ireland trace their descent from Walter de Clifford, who took the name from Clifford (Castle) in Herefordshire in the 12th century, after acquiring the Clifford barony by marriage.

variants.name: Collman
meanings: English (Middlesex and Surrey): variant of   Coleman  .
Americanized form of German   Kollmann  , or of   Kohlmann  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.description: The Virginia Company founded the first permanent English colony at Jamestown in 1607. One of the original 13 states (it entered the Union in 1788). Virginia holds an important place in American history, as it was home to many of the founding fathers, including George Washington, Thomas Jefferson, James Madison, George Mason, and Patrick Henry. Four of Americas first five presidents were Virginians. During the Civil War, Richmond, Virginias capital, was the capital of the Confederacy. Today, Virginia is a popular tourist spot where people can visit historic places such as Alexandria, Williamsburg, and Mount Vernon, George Washingtons estate.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard Coleman was born on 30 May 1590, in Great Braxted, Essex, England, his father, Sir James Coleman, was 21 and his mother, Mary Elizabeth Spencer, was 19. He married Alice Sowtherne on 25 June 1611, in Mickleton, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died on 13 May 1679, in Brent Eleigh, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 88, and was buried in Brent Eleigh, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Coleman è nato il il 30 maggio 1590 nel Great Braxted, Essex, England, il suo padre, Sir James Coleman, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Mary Elizabeth Spencer, aveva 19 anni. Lui sposato Alice Sowtherne il 25 giugno 1611, nel Mickleton, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì il 13 maggio 1679, nel Brent Eleigh, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 88 anni, e fu sepolto a Brent Eleigh, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Colemanが1590年5月30日にイギリスエセックスグレアト ブラクステドで生まれた時，父Sir James Colemanは21歳，母Mary Elizabeth Spencerは19歳であった。1611年6月25日にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアミックレトンでAlice Sowtherneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1679年5月13日にイギリスイギリススフォルクブレント エレイグで88歳で死去し，イギリスイギリススフォルクブレント エレイグにて埋葬された。

ko.Essex, Virginia, British Colonial Americ: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 에세스
ko.Gloucester Point, Gloucester, Virginia,: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 그루스스터 그루스스터 포인트
ko.All Saints Church, Great Braxted, Essex,: 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 그리트 브라스테드 앨 세인츠 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Coleman(이)가 1590년 5월 30일 영국 에세스 그리트 브라스테드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir James Coleman(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Elizabeth Spencer(은)는 19세였다. 그는 1611년 6월 25일 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 믹레턴에서 Alice Sowtherne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1679년 5월 13일 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 브렌트 엘리에서 88세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 브렌트 엘리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Coleman nasceu em 30 de maio de 1590, em Great Braxted, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir James Coleman, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Mary Elizabeth Spencer, tinha 19 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice Sowtherne em 25 de junho de 1611, em Mickleton, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 13 de maio de 1679, em Brent Eleigh, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 88 anos, e foi sepultado em Brent Eleigh, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard Coleman родился 30 мая 1590 г. в местности грит бракстед, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir James Coleman было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Mary Elizabeth Spencer, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Sowtherne 25 июня 1611 г. в местности микклетон, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер 13 мая 1679 г. в местности брент элиг, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 88 лет и был похоронен в местности брент элиг, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Richard Coleman nació el 30 de mayo de 1590, en Great Braxted, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir James Coleman, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Mary Elizabeth Spencer, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Alice Sowtherne el 25 de junio de 1611, en Mickleton, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió el 13 de mayo de 1679, en Brent Eleigh, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 88 años, y fue sepultado en Brent Eleigh, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
